title: library-architecture
purpose: Use for core text-analysis, measurement, line-breaking, bidi metadata, and public layout API changes in the Pretext engine.
when: When changing Pretext's core engine (prepare, layout, analysis, measurement, line-break, bidi, inline-flow) or public API surface.

title: development-workflow
purpose: Use for setup, command selection, local iteration, page navigation, and the standard Pretext contributor loop.
when: When setting up the Pretext dev environment, choosing commands, iterating locally, or checking status dashboards.

title: browser-accuracy
purpose: Use for browser sweep work, accuracy claims, mismatch diagnosis, and refresh rules for checked-in accuracy snapshots.
when: When running browser accuracy checks, diagnosing cross-browser mismatches, or refreshing accuracy snapshots.

title: benchmarks-profiling
purpose: Use for hot-path performance work, benchmark regressions, CPU profiling, allocation churn, and retained-memory checks.
when: When profiling Pretext performance, diagnosing benchmark regressions, or checking allocation and memory behavior.

title: corpus-diagnostics
purpose: Use for long-form corpus canaries, width sweeps, probes, extractor questions, font matrices, and mismatch taxonomy work.
when: When running corpus sweeps, diagnosing text-shaping mismatches, or working with script-sensitive canary texts.

title: packaging-release
purpose: Use for published package shape, dist output, entrypoints, build correctness, and release confidence checks.
when: When building dist/, verifying package exports, running smoke tests, or preparing a release.

title: demo-dogfooding
purpose: Use for building, updating, or validating demos that exercise the rich line APIs and editorial-layout capabilities.
when: When building or updating Pretext demos, exercising rich line APIs, or dogfooding editorial layout features.

title: priorities-roadmap
purpose: Use for deciding what to work on next, understanding active canaries, and knowing what not to reopen.
when: When deciding what to work on, checking active priorities, or avoiding reopening resolved or deprioritized work.

title: research-log
purpose: Use for interpreting earlier experiments, rejected hypotheses, and durable conclusions that should shape future work.
when: When proposing architecture changes, revisiting shaping-heavy work, or separating durable lessons from abandoned experiments.

title: architecture-review
purpose: Use for repo review, contributor audits, change-risk reviews, and system-level decision quality assessment.
when: When reviewing proposed diffs, auditing contributors, assessing change risk, or checking system-level decision quality.
